    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.00 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 442.32 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [HIGH] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 258.29 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 442.32 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.00 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5501 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy image from administracion.gob.es (est. 487.14 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.

    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.

    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.

    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 70 small targets on mobile and 119 on tablet.

    - 4 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:

    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:

    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 683.28 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 412.36 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 29.76 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 683.28 KB.
  - [HIGH] Right-size images for viewport
    - Avoid sending larger images than the rendered size needs. Estimated byte savings: 475.19 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 442.32 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 412.36 KB.
  - [HIGH] Lazy-load offscreen images
    - Defer below-the-fold imagery until it is likely to be viewed. Estimated byte savings: 258.29 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.00 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL

    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.

    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.

  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.

    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5395 ms.

    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 40 small targets on mobile and 66 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 1 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.61 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 372.07 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.86 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5364 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 999.74 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.8 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)

  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images and media galleries (est. 102.47 KB)
    - Load imagery only when it enters the viewport or when a user opens the relevant section.
  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 563.42 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical UI modules until after first render
    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.
  - [MEDIUM] Modularize heavy script asset from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB)
    - Load this script only on pages or interactions that require it; prefer dynamic import and feature-level bundles.
- External CSS/JS hosting checks:
  - SCRIPT www.googletagmanager.com: GREEN (Google Cloud); 2 request(s), 278.11 KB
- Third-party JS recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Maintain a third-party JavaScript inventory
    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.88 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 563.42 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 102.47 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 563.42 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.88 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5065 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.52 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 75 small targets on mobile and 108 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 7 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 2 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 2 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.42 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.88 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5065 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 872.07 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 6.9 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index

  - WSG: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance)

  - WSG: 2 Remove unnecessary or redundant information (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#remove-unnecessary-or-redundant-information)
- [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources

  - WSG: 18 Use the most efficient solution for your service (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-the-most-efficient-solution-for-your-service)

  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 37 small targets on mobile and 75 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 5 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.77 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.19 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4797 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 851.73 KB).

    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.84 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 563.67 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 114.20 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 563.67 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.84 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 5068 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [HIGH] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy script from administracion.gob.es (est. 248.51 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 41 small targets on mobile and 72 on tablet.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 2 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 2 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.67 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.84 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5068 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.

    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL

    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.

    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.

    - Defer below-the-fold imagery until it is likely to be viewed. Estimated byte savings: 127.76 KB.

    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.

    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.

    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.

    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.83 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.

    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.

    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.

    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.

    - 2 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 2 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.

  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 562.54 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.83 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5103 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 870.59 KB).

    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.

    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.

    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 567.16 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.86 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5092 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.

    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.

    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.

    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 564.68 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.88 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4522 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 863.45 KB).
- [HIGH] Largest Contentful Paint
  - Current value: 7.3 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)
- [HIGH] Speed Index

    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 39 small targets on mobile and 66 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 2 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 1 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add missing expected site files
    - Missing expected file(s): /robots.txt.
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 569.34 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.16 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 5056 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 852.34 KB).

    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.

  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.81 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4589 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.

  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 837.40 KB).

    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 563.43 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.20 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4829 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.

  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.

    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL

    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 54 small targets on mobile and 84 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 3 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 2 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 2 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 568.10 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.84 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4759 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 891.41 KB).

    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.

    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.

    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.

    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.

    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.

    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 168.18 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.

    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.

    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.

- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 1 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.

    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 567.96 KB.
  - [HIGH] Enable text compression
    - Serve text assets with Brotli or gzip to remove avoidable transfer bloat. Estimated byte savings: 285.11 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 168.18 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 4562 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 851.75 KB).

  - [HIGH] Split JavaScript by route or interaction (est. 567.40 KB)
    - Move non-critical bundles behind route-level or event-driven imports so they are only fetched on demand.

    - Keep only above-the-fold resources in the initial path; hydrate or import secondary modules later.

    - Split this stylesheet so component-specific CSS is delivered only when the component is rendered.

- Third-party JS recommendations:

    - Track 1 third-party script origin(s) and assign an owner plus business justification for each.
  - [MEDIUM] Reduce third-party script payload and requests
    - Defer non-critical tags, remove unused vendors, and gate scripts behind consent or user interaction.
- Dead-code recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS selectors and component styles (est. 167.91 KB savings)
    - Trim styles that never match rendered markup and split component CSS so only used styles are shipped.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript and feature bundles (est. 567.40 KB savings)
    - Drop unused packages and feature code, or move them behind route-level or interaction-triggered imports.
- Non-critical loading candidates:
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images (est. 122.83 KB deferrable)
    - Load below-the-fold images only when they approach the viewport.
  - [HIGH] Defer non-critical JavaScript (est. 567.40 KB deferrable)
    - Split bundles and load feature code on interaction or route transition.
  - [HIGH] Inline critical CSS and defer remainder (est. 167.91 KB deferrable)
    - Ship only above-the-fold CSS in initial render path and defer non-critical styles.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources (est. 3445 ms unblock)
    - Defer scripts/styles not required for first paint and preload only truly critical assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Evaluate deferred loading for heavy stylesheet from administracion.gob.es (est. 171.98 KB deferrable)
    - Confirm this asset is required for first paint; defer or lazy-load if not critical.
- Form validation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Missing associated label
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] No error messaging hook
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
  - [MEDIUM] Missing autocomplete for tooling support
    - 1 unique form control signature(s) on this page include this issue.
- Metadata recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add canonical URL metadata
    - Use rel=canonical to reduce duplicate URL ambiguity for crawlers and tooling.
  - [MEDIUM] Complete Open Graph metadata
    - Include og:title, og:description, og:type, and og:url for robust machine-readable sharing metadata.
  - [LOW] Add twitter:card metadata
    - Include twitter card metadata to improve cross-platform machine-readable previews.
- Layout adaptation recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Increase touch target sizes
    - Ensure interactive controls are at least 44x44 CSS px. Detected 55 small targets on mobile and 79 on tablet.
  - [MEDIUM] Refactor fixed-width components
    - 1 element(s) exceeded viewport width in tested layouts; use responsive component constraints.
- Lightweight security recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add a Content-Security-Policy header
    - Add an appropriate CSP to reduce script injection and resource loading risk.
  - [LOW] Define a Permissions-Policy
    - Restrict browser capabilities to only what the application needs.
  - [MEDIUM] Add SRI to external scripts
    - 2 external script tag(s) are missing integrity attributes.
- Dependency maintenance recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add integrity attributes for external dependencies
    - 2 external script dependency URL(s) are missing SRI integrity attributes.
- Language/runtime version recommendations:
  - [LOW] No obvious outdated runtime version disclosed
    - No clearly outdated runtime version signals were exposed in sampled HTTP headers. Verify language/runtime versions in source and CI since backend versions are not always externally visible.
- Expected-files recommendations:
  - [MEDIUM] Add beneficial discoverability and maintenance files
    - Missing beneficial file(s): /.well-known/security.txt, /manifest.webmanifest, /favicon.ico.
- Compression recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS before transfer
    - Minification amplifies compression efficiency and reduces payload size. Estimated savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Use modern image compression formats
    - Prefer AVIF/WebP over legacy formats where possible to reduce transfer size. Estimated savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Enable Brotli/gzip for text assets
    - Compress HTML, CSS, JS, JSON, SVG, and XML responses with Brotli or gzip. Estimated savings: 65.70 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Minify JavaScript before transfer
    - Minify JS bundles so transport compression produces better results with fewer bytes. Estimated savings: 62.26 KB.
  - [LOW] Improve image encoding quality/size
    - Re-encode oversized images and tune compression quality for significant byte savings. Estimated savings: 7.37 KB.
- Optimization recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Remove unused JavaScript
    - Reduce dead JS and split bundles to avoid shipping non-critical code. Estimated byte savings: 567.40 KB.
  - [HIGH] Remove unused CSS
    - Trim unused selectors and split CSS so only needed styles are shipped. Estimated byte savings: 167.91 KB.
  - [HIGH] Minify CSS
    - Use minified production CSS output to reduce bytes in transit. Estimated byte savings: 130.31 KB.
  - [HIGH] Eliminate render-blocking resources
    - Inline critical assets and defer non-critical scripts/styles for faster rendering. Estimated render unblocking: 3445 ms.
  - [MEDIUM] Serve images in next-gen formats
    - Convert high-byte images to more efficient formats such as AVIF or WebP. Estimated byte savings: 123.71 KB.
  - [MEDIUM] Lazy-load offscreen images
    - Defer below-the-fold imagery until it is likely to be viewed. Estimated byte savings: 122.83 KB.
- Offline/caching recommendations:
  - [HIGH] Add service worker support
    - Register a service worker to support resilient caching and offline behavior for key routes.
  - [HIGH] Ensure critical routes work offline
    - Provide an offline fallback strategy for key pages and shell assets.
  - [MEDIUM] Improve web app manifest support
    - Complete manifest fields so clients can install and run an app-like experience.
  - [MEDIUM] Support offline start URL behavior
    - Ensure start URL and shell routing can load in disconnected conditions.
  - [MEDIUM] Strengthen static asset cache TTL
    - Improve long-lived cache headers for immutable static assets (estimated savings opportunity: 595.92 KB).

  - Current value: 4.9 s
  - WSG: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact (https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact)

## Copy/Paste Issue Snippets

```md
## Improve Largest Contentful Paint

- Lighthouse audit: largest-contentful-paint
- Current value: 15.0 s
- WSG guideline: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact
- WSG link: https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact

### Why this matters
Largest Contentful Paint marks the time at which the largest text or image is painted. [Learn more about the Largest Contentful Paint metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/lighthouse-largest-contentful-paint/)

### Definition of done
- [ ] Metric improves in Lighthouse on key templates
- [ ] Changes documented in release notes
- [ ] Re-scan confirms improvement
```

```md
## Improve Speed Index

- Lighthouse audit: speed-index
- Current value: 9.5 s
- WSG guideline: 1 Set goals based on performance and energy impact
- WSG link: https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#set-goals-based-on-performance-and-energy-impact

### Why this matters
Speed Index shows how quickly the contents of a page are visibly populated. [Learn more about the Speed Index metric](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/speed-index/).

### Definition of done
- [ ] Metric improves in Lighthouse on key templates
- [ ] Changes documented in release notes
- [ ] Re-scan confirms improvement
```

```md
## Improve Reduce unused JavaScript

- Lighthouse audit: unused-javascript
- Current value: Est savings of 442 KiB
- WSG guideline: 16 Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance
- WSG link: https://www.w3.org/TR/web-sustainability-guidelines/#use-dependencies-appropriately-and-ensure-maintenance

### Why this matters
Reduce unused JavaScript and defer loading scripts until they are required to decrease bytes consumed by network activity. [Learn how to reduce unused JavaScript](https://developer.chrome.com/docs/lighthouse/performance/unused-javascript/).

### Definition of done
- [ ] Metric improves in Lighthouse on key templates
- [ ] Changes documented in release notes
- [ ] Re-scan confirms improvement
```
